2605.29971.title: Causal Interventions on Continuous Variables: A Case Study on Verb Bias in Steering Vectors for In-Context Learning
2605.29971.paper_summary: Causal interventions in language model representations have largely targeted discrete features, like grammatical number. However, language models must also make use of features that are graded. We introduce a method for causal intervention on continuous variables: given activation vectors paired with a graded target variable, we localize a low-dimensional direction for that variable and use this direction to edit a vectors toward counterfactual target values. We apply this method to a continuous feature that is well-studied in psycholinguistics, namely verb bias (which reflects which syntactic structures tend to follow a given verb). We show that verb bias is causally represented in steering vectors extracted from large language models: counterfactual edits to verb bias systematically shift downstream structural preferences. Verb bias has also previously been linked to in-context learning; in further analyses, we find that steering vectors encode error signals that could drive the error-driven update behavior seen in in-context learning but that these aspects of the steering vectors are not causally used in downstream production. Overall, these results show causal interventions can be applied to continuous variables, though connecting continuous variables to in-context learning remains a challenge.
2605.29971.paper_summary_zh: 因果干預在語言模型表示中的應用主要針對離散特徵，例如語法數量。<lb/>然而，語言模型也必須利用一些連續的特徵。<lb/>我們提出了一種對連續變數進行因果干預的方法：給定與分級目標變數配對的激活向量，我們為該變數定位一個低維方向，並利用這個方向來編輯向量，以達到反事實的目標值。<lb/>我們將這種方法應用於心理語言學中研究良好的連續特徵，即動詞偏向（反映了哪些句法結構傾向於跟隨給定的動詞）。<lb/>我們顯示動詞偏向在從大型語言模型中提取的引導向量中具有因果表示：對動詞偏向的反事實編輯系統性地改變下游結構偏好。<lb/>動詞偏向之前也與上下文學習相關聯；在進一步的分析中，我們發現引導向量編碼了可能驅動上下文學習中所見的錯誤驅動更新行為的錯誤信號，但這些引導向量的這些方面在下游生成中並未被因果使用。<lb/>總體而言，這些結果顯示因果干預可以應用於連續變數，儘管將連續變數與上下文學習聯繫起來仍然是一個挑戰。
2605.29971.author: Zhenghao Herbert Zhou et.al.
2605.29971.authors: Zhenghao Herbert Zhou, R. Thomas McCoy, Robert Frank
